close_reason: Added NVM_DATA_MAX const, early-return size check in put() with clear error message, documented limit in rustdoc
description: wolfhsm nvm_add and nvm_overwrite validate u16::try_from(data.len()) and return Error::BadArgs for values >= 65536 bytes (wolfhsm-0.1.0/src/nvm.rs:343-417). put() in secretx-wolfhsm passes the full SecretValue bytes through and surfaces this as SecretError::Backend('invalid argument: nvm_add data exceeds u16::MAX bytes'). Neither the README nor rustdoc mention the limit, so users putting large blobs (TLS private bundle, multi-cert chain) hit a backend error with no hint that the cause is size. Fix: (a) document the 65535-byte limit in README and rustdoc, (b) check value.len() in put() before calling into wolfhsm and return SecretError::InvalidUri or a more specific error noting the limit.
title: Undocumented 65535-byte secret value size limit; large inputs return opaque Backend error
